'''Liam Marshall''' (born 9 May 1996) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]] and {{rlnt|England}}.

He has spent time on loan from [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[Swinton Lions]] in the [[2016 RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Marshall was born in [[Lancashire, England]].

==Playing career ==

===Wigan Warriors===

====Swinton Lions (loan)====
Marshall spent the 2016 season on loan at [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] side [[Swinton Lions]]. He made a number of impressive performances at the club, most notably scoring four tries in a 38–35 win against Halifax,<ref>{{cite web |title=Halifax 35 Swinton Lions 38 - Liam Marshall denies Halifax victory with winning try |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/halifax-35-swinton-lions-38-liam-marshall-denies-halifax-victory-winning-try-1800345 |website=Yorkshire Post |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=11 April 2016}}</ref> and a hat trick in a 40–38 win against [[Sheffield Eagles]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Sheffield Eagles 38 Swinton 40: Winning run ends for crisis club Eagles |url=https://www.thestar.co.uk/news/sheffield-eagles-38-swinton-40-winning-run-ends-for-crisis-club-eagles-455256 |website=The Star |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=21 August 2016}}</ref> He finished the year as the club's top try scorer with 20 tries in 22 appearances.<ref>{{cite web |title=Swinton Lions - 2016 |url=https://stats.rugbyleaguerecords.com/teamseason.php?cat=25&subcat=2016 |website=Rugby League Records |access-date=6 April 2023}}</ref>

====Return to parent club====

He made his first team début on 3 March 2017, against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|author= |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP3983222 |title=Wigan Warriors v Leigh Centurions - Rugby League |publisher= [[BBC Sport]] |date=1970-01-01 |accessdate=2017-03-05}}</ref> Marshall also scored four tries on just his second game for the Warriors in a 38–16 win at the [[Warrington Wolves]] on 9 March 2017.<ref>{{cite web |title=Marshall's four tries lead Wigan to victory |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/39552/marshalls-four-tries-lead-wigan-to-victory |website=rugby-league.com |publisher=Rugby Football League |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=9 March 2017}}</ref> He played in the [[2017 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/challenge-cup-final-live-score-11054626|title=Hull FC 18-14 Wigan recap as the Black and Whites claim back-to-back Challenge Cup triumphs|publisher=Mirror|date=26 August 2017|accessdate=6 November 2019}}</ref>

Marshall made 22 appearances for Wigan in the 2018 season and scored 17 tries, but his season was cut short by an [[anterior cruciate ligament]] injury in August 2018.<ref>{{cite web |title=Marshall suffers injury in Wigan Warriors' victory at Catalans |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/marshall-suffers-injury-wigan-warriors-victory-catalans-264802 |website=Wigan Today |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=18 August 2018}}</ref> He missed the club's [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/45800241 |title = Super League Grand Final: Wigan claim fifth title with victory over Warrington|date=13 October 2018|work=BBC Sport|access-date=17 October 2018}}</ref>

Marshall played 23 games for Wigan in the 2019 season and scored 16 tries including Wigan's shock loss to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the semi-final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23–6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref>

Marshall made 10 appearances for Wigan in the 2020 season, scoring six tries, but was ruled out for the rest of the season after suffering another serious knee injury in a match against [[Castleford Tigers]] on 29 August 2020.<ref>{{cite web |title=Liam Marshall: Wigan Warriors winger out for 9-12 months with knee injury |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54017346 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=4 September 2020}}</ref> As a result, he missed the club's Grand Final loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Marshall scored two tries in a 24-10 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/match-report-hull-kr-wigan-warriors-3565355/|title=Wigan Warriors start their Super League campaign with a 24-10 win away to Hull KR|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Marshall scored the winning try for Wigan in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk|date=26 May 2022 }}</ref>
In round 21, Marshall scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 46-4 victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12660812/super-league-bevan-french-and-liam-marshall-star-for-wigan-warriors-in-46-4-win-over-hull-kr/|title=Super League: Bevan French and Liam Marshall star for Wigan Warriors in 46-4 win over Hull KR|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

In round 2 of the 2023 Super League season, Marshall scored four tries for Wigan in a 60–0 victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12818901/wigan-warriors-60-0-wakefield-trinity-liam-marshall-scores-four-tries-as-wigan-run-riot-against-wakefield/|title=Wigan Warriors 60-0 Wakefield Trinity: Liam Marshall scores four tries as Wigan run riot against Wakefield|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref> a match in which he also scored his 100th career try for the club.<ref>{{cite web |title=Liam Marshall reflects on his 100th try for Wigan Warriors |url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/liam-marshall-reflects-on-his-100th-try-for-wigan-warriors-4045926 |website=Wigan Today |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=1 March 2023}}</ref>
The following week, Marshall scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 36–0 victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-castleford-tigers-key-26389491/|title=Key talking points from Wigan Warriors' win over Castleford Tigers as Liam Marshall stakes England claim|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, Marshall played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]] scoring the only try of the match.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Marshall scored two tries in Wigan's 32-4 victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 24 February 2024, Marshall played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|date=24 February 2024|work=[[BBC Sport]]}}</ref>
On 1 March 2024, Marshall scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 30-16 victory over Huddersfield.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/av/rugby-league/68456639|title=Wigan Warriors 30-16 Huddersfield Giants: Liam Marshall scores hat-trick in hosts' win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Marshall played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref> Marshall signed a new four-year contract with Wigan in August 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://wiganwarriors.com/blog/2024/08/02/marshall-signs-new-four-year-deal/|title=Marshall signs new four-year deal|website=[[Wigan Warriors]]}}</ref> Marshall made the [[Super League Dream Team]] for the [[2024 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c78de9v0q21o|title=Wigan head up Super League Dream Team selections|date=22 September 2024|work=[[BBC Sport]]|access-date=15 May 2025}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Marshall played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|date=12 Oct 2024|last=Calvert|first=Lee|work=[[The Guardian]]}}</ref>

In 2025, Marshall kept his place in the [[Super League Dream Team]].<ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce800g1e0l4o</ref><ref>https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/2025-super-league-dream-team-named-including-two-shock-omissions-as-hull-kr-dominate-picks</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Marshall played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International==
On 2 Nov 2024 he made his début for {{rlnt|England}} in the 34-16 win over {{rlnt|Samoa}}, also scoring his 1st try.
